name: Valera de Abajo from Alarcón — circular by the shore of the Embalse Alarcón
name_original: Valera de Abajo desde Alarcón — circular por la orilla del Embalse Alarcón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alarcón features routes that navigate the varied landscape of the region. The area is characterized by the Júcar River, its gorges, and the expansive Embalse Alarcón reservoir. Terrain includes paths along the water's edge, through agricultural areas, and over undulating hills. This provides a mix of flat sections and more challenging ascents for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alarcón The most popular touring cycling route is Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the scenic Júcar River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valera de Abajo from Alarcón — circular by the shore of the Embalse Alarcón, a difficult 48.4 miles (77.9 km) path. This route offers extensive views along the reservoir's shore. Local touring cyclists also love the Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar, a 49.9 miles (80.3 km) trail leading through river gorges and past the Contreras Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours. Touring cycling around Alarcón is defined by river gorges, large reservoirs, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy touring bike trails near Alarcón to more advanced cycling routes. The routes in Alarcón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alarcón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alarcón?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in the Alarcón area, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alarcón?
A: Yes, Alarcón offers 3 easy touring cycling routes. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363965751/jucar-river-gorge-trail-from-el-picazo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.7 miles (17.2 km) long and follows the scenic Júcar River.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Alarcón?
A: Yes, many routes in the Alarcón area are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086183277/valera-de-abajo-from-alarcon-circular-by-the-shore-of-the-embalse-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valera de Abajo from Alarcón — circular by the shore of the Embalse Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views along the reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365716918/paracuellos-y-almodovar-del-pinar-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; is another popular loop.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in Alarcón?
A: Route lengths vary significantly, from shorter rides like the 10.7-mile (17.2 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363965751/jucar-river-gorge-trail-from-el-picazo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt; (approx. 1 hour 35 minutes) to longer, more challenging routes such as the 49.9-mile (80.3 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, which can take around 5 hours.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alarcón?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always available, the easier routes, particularly those with less elevation gain and away from heavy traffic, are generally suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1363965751/jucar-river-gorge-trail-from-el-picazo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Gorge Trail from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt; is a good starting point due to its easy difficulty and scenic river path.

Q: What kind of attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Alarcón?
A: The routes often pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de La Losa on the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;, or cross the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt; and the Embalse Alarcón reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Alarcón?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Alarcón, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months.

Q: Are there advanced touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Alarcón has 13 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, with its significant elevation gain, is a prime example of a challenging route through river gorges and past reservoirs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alarcón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from river gorges to expansive reservoirs, and the mix of flat sections and challenging ascents that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through towns or villages where I can find cafes or stops?
A: Many routes in the Alarcón area connect or pass near local towns and villages, offering opportunities for refreshment. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086241247/alarcon-circular-from-motilla-de-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón — circular from Motilla de Palancar&lt;/a&gt; will take you through the town of Alarcón itself, where you can find amenities. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify such stops along the way.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Alarcón?
A: Parking availability varies by starting point. For routes beginning in larger towns like Motilla del Palancar or El Picazo, you can typically find public parking areas. When planning your tour on komoot, you can often see suggested starting points and parking options near the route's beginning.

Q: Is it possible to rent a touring bicycle in Alarcón?
A: While Alarcón is a smaller town, nearby larger towns or cities in the region may offer bicycle rental services. It is advisable to check with local tourism offices or sports shops in advance, especially if you are looking for specific touring bicycle models.
tips: From this viewpoint you will have the best views of the gorges of the Júcar river on its way around the town of Alarcón. You will also be able to appreciate the castle of the town, a true postcard.
A monument to honor the town of Gascas submerged under the waters of the Alarcón Reservoir. When the water level in the swamp is low you can see the walls of the abandoned houses.
A tower-fort from the 14th-14th centuries. Built on a high place, it served as a place of surveillance. After restoration works it is open for visitors.

waypoints.name: Monument to the Gasqueños of Gascas
